articles.title: Govt pattas undercut crackdown on illegal colonies
articles.description: Rajasthan govt issues pattas via Urban Service Camps to regularise unauthorised colonies, easing inspections—officials warn it encourages encroachments and unsafe layouts.

articles.title: Saudi Crown Prince MBS ‘Guarantees’ Iran’s $24 Billion Payday, Trump ‘Loses’ Gulf Ally? | Report
articles.description: Saudi Arabia is emerging as a key player in the evolving Iran-US agreement. Reports claim Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has privately assured Tehran that billions of dollars owed under the deal will be paid, even if political hurdles emerge in Washington. The revelation comes as the Trump administration insists that any released Iranian funds will remain under strict US oversight. Is Riyadh positioning itself as the ultimate power broker in the Middle East? Watch the full report as the battle over Iran's frozen assets, US leverage, and Saudi influence intensifies.

articles.title: Who is Vinod Doddamani? Indian-origin lawyer facing $250,000 fine over asylum fraud in US
articles.description: ICE has moved to fine Indian-origin immigration attorney Vinod Doddamani over alleged fraudulent asylum filings and false documents in US courts.

articles.title: West Delhi school rape case: HC to pass order on cancelling bail of accused on June 29
articles.description: The Delhi High Court on Wednesday said it will pass an order on June 29 on whether to cancel the bail granted to the 57-year-old caretaker accused of raping a three-year-old girl inside a private school in west Delhi. A Vacation Bench of Justice Vinod Kumar reserved its verdict on the petitions by Delhi Police and the complainant against the trial court’s May 7 order releasing Lalit Kumar on bail in the case registered under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.

articles.title: Paper marriage faces legal test; Gujarat High Court steps in
articles.description: Sets Aside Prosecution Order Against Couple Seeking To Invalidate Marriage Registered Using Engagement Photographs As ‘Proof’

articles.title: HC stays return of abused dogs to owner
articles.description: Observing that “the arc of justice must extend even to those who cannot approach the courts themselves”, the High Court of Karnataka set aside a trial court order directing that nine abused dogs be handed back to their alleged tormentor during the pendency of the investigation. It called the order “preposterous to say the least” and one that “shocks the conscience of the court.”

articles.title: Madhu Bangarappa objects to title, content of NCERT class 6 ‘R3’ Kannada textbook
articles.description: Former School Education Minister Madhu Bangarappa has expressed strong objections to the title of the National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) class 6 ‘R3’ Kannada textbook and its controversial content.

articles.title: ‘I Never Asked Trump’: Defiant Netanyahu’s Stunning Iran War Confession As Rift With US Explodes
articles.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has made a stunning admission about Israel’s confrontation with Iran, revealing that he informed U.S. President Donald Trump of his plans rather than seeking permission. In a defiant speech, Netanyahu said he was guided by the Israeli people, not Washington, and vowed that Iran would never obtain nuclear weapons. He also doubled down on Israel’s military operations in Gaza, Lebanon, Syria, and Rafah, insisting that security comes through strength, not diplomacy. The remarks are likely to fuel debate over U.S.-Israel relations, regional security, and the future of the Middle East.

articles.title: NFHS-6 figures exposes Gujarat govt’s claims of women’s empowerment: Congress
articles.description: NFHS-6 figures exposes Gujarat govt’s claims of women’s empowerment: Congress

articles.title: Trump Dramatically Cancels Housing Signing Amid New Medical Speculation
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to postpone the signing of a major housing bill has triggered a wave of political reactions, including controversial comments from Representative Ted Lieu, who questioned whether the POTUS had received a special drug treatment and called on the White House to clarify the situation. This video examines Trump's announcement, the status of the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, and the political controversy surrounding Lieu's remarks.

articles.title: 'Everybody’s sick of you': New book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during heated Gaza ceasefire call
articles.description: A new book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during Gaza ceasefire talks in 2025. The reported outburst exposed deep private tensions despite their public alliance.

articles.title: China to enforce new ethnic unity law globally, says it has right to target 'separatists'
articles.description: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which comes into force on July 1, seeks to strengthen what Beijing describes as a shared national identity among the country's ethnic groups. One provision extends potential legal liability to people and organisations outside China, an aspect that has drawn particular scrutiny. Rights advocates warn that the measure could provide fresh legal grounds for targeting overseas campaigners from communities such as the Uyghurs and Tibetans.

articles.title: `When Villages Progress, Punjab Progresses`: CM Bhagwant Singh Mann
articles.description: The AAP Chief said the decision reflects the Bhagwant Mann Government’s commitment to empowering grassroots democracy, strengthening rural governance and accelerating village development, as the government continues to take people-centric decisions aimed at building a prosperous and Rangla Punjab.

articles.title: 19 BJP councillors retake oath in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation after Kerala HC invalidates earlier ceremony
articles.description: 19 BJP councillors in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation have retaken their oaths following a High Court ruling that invalidated their previous ones. The court mandated strict adherence to the prescribed format, disallowing any additions like deities or "Mother India." This decision stems from challenges to oaths taken by 20 BJP councillors who had invoked Hindu deities and political martyrs. The BJP currently holds a majority in the 101-member civic body.

articles.title: ‘EVERYBODY HATES YOU!’: Trump Explodes At Netanyahu With ‘DIVORCE’ Threat In Secret Call | Report
articles.description: Did Donald Trump threaten to 'divorce' Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu during an angry phone call? A new book is shedding light on an alleged private clash between the two leaders, claiming what could be one of the most serious rifts in their long-standing political relationship. According to Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, Trump erupted during a September 2025 phone call over Netanyahu’s reluctance to accept a U.S.-brokered Gaza ceasefire plan. The book claims Trump told Netanyahu that “everybody hates you” and warned that continued opposition to the deal could lead to a “divorce” between the United States and Israel. The conversation reportedly took place in the presence of Trump envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner.

articles.title: CPI(M) urges Centre to withdraw move to include inhabited areas of Western Ghats in ESAs
articles.description: The CPI(M) State secretariat has urged the Central government to withdraw its move to include inhabited areas in the Western Ghats under the list of Eco-Sensitive Areas (ESA).

articles.title: Trump’s Smart Iran Exit
articles.description: It wasn’t getting better for POTUS, so he was right to end the war. Plus, Americans have grown tired of US’s West Asian conflicts. This has implications for US-Israel ties. Tehran is emboldened, but managing the new regional architecture will require India’s involvement

articles.title: Kerala High Court rejects plea challenging election of K.C. Venugopal in 2024 Lok Sabha elections
articles.description: Kerala High Court dismisses A.M. Ariff's challenge to K.C. Venugopal's 2024 election win, citing procedural defects and lack of evidence.

articles.title: Delhi HC allows 15-year-old rape survivor to terminate 26-week pregnancy
articles.description: The Delhi High Court permitted a 15-year-old rape survivor to terminate her 26-week pregnancy based on a medical board's report. AIIMS will bear the cost, and the court has ordered the foetus to be preserved for DNA testing.

articles.title: US-Iran deal is 'America's declaration of defeat', says Ghalibaf
articles.description: "That is why, the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding became a declaration of America's defeat," he said.

articles.title: Calcutta High Court refuses urgent hearing in cases relating to Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee
articles.description: Calcutta High Court denies urgent hearing for Abhishek Banerjee's medical travel, citing ongoing cases and normal legal procedures.

articles.description: Nana Kwaku Bonsam, one of Ghana's most controversial spiritual figures, has resurfaced with claims that he influenced England captain Harry Kane after previously asserting he was behind Cristiano...

articles.title: Child marriage curb: Maharashtra considering a rule mandating birth dates on wedding cards
articles.description: Maharashtra is exploring printing birth dates on wedding invitations to combat child marriages, aiming to reduce incidents to under 10% in five years. The state is studying Rajasthan's successful model. While child marriage rates have declined, authorities have intervened in over 1,400 cases recently, registering numerous FIRs. Enhanced detection and intervention are credited for increased prevention numbers, with efforts also targeting priests and facilitators.

articles.title: Israel could hand over territory to Lebanon under US-backed proposal
articles.description: The proposed "pilot" project is being discussed and is in the latest rounds of talks between Lebanese and Israeli officials, which began in Washington on Tuesday.

articles.title: Can childhood explain a monster? What Jeffrey Epstein's early years reveal and what they don't
articles.description: A New York Times investigation traces Jeffrey Epstein's childhood, friendships and family life, searching for clues to one of history's most notorious sexual predators. The answers remain unsettlingly incomplete.

articles.title: Iran’s Qalibaf calls US-Iran agreement ‘America’s declaration of defeat’
articles.description: Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, recently described a new understanding as proof of America's failure, attributing this victory to the steadfastness of Iran rather than external pressures. In his remarks made in Baku, he insisted that the nations of the region should control the future of Middle Eastern security, dismissing the role of foreign powers.

articles.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk finds clarity in speaking out on war and values
articles.description: BANGALORE, June 24 : Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk said taking a strong stance against Russia's invasion of her country had sharpened her sense of purpose on the tour, with the online backlash she receives deepening her resolve to keep speaking out.Kostyuk has been one of the most outspoken players on the war that

articles.title: TMC Split Deepens: Rebels Remove Mamata, Stake Claim To Party Symbol
articles.description: The battle for control of the Trinamool Congress has intensified, with the Ritabrata Banerjee-led rebel faction removing Mamata Banerjee as party chairperson, suspending Abhishek Banerjee, and announcing a new National Executive Committee. The rebels have approached the Election Commission, claiming they are the "real" TMC and enjoy majority support among party MLAs. In response, the Mamata camp has reaffirmed its organizational structure and leadership before the poll panel. The crisis has deepened further with former Kolkata Mayor Firhad Hakim joining the rebel camp. As rival factions battle over the party's name, symbol and leadership, the final decision may rest with constitutional authorities and the courts.

articles.title: Madras High Court mandates ‘X,Y’ anonymity for couples alleging mental illness in matrimonial cases
articles.description: The Madras High Court observed that public disclosure of such persons' identities could lead to psychological injury, social ostracism, enduring emotional trauma and lifelong stigma, which can legitimately be avoided.

articles.description: As a crushing election defeat punctures the welfare-led ‘Diamond Harbour Model’, the sharp-suited Senapati faces a raw, old-school street rebellion from his own party

articles.title: OPINION | Justice Delayed, Democracy Denied: The Madras High Court's wake-up call on election petitions
articles.description: The Madras High Court highlighted the dangers of delays in election cases. It called for faster resolution and dedicated benches to protect democratic representation

articles.description: US Senate votes to restrict military action against Iran, signaling a split in the GOP. Read how Trump reacted to the setback and what it means for 2026 policy.

articles.title: Zohran Mamdani-backed candidates excel in congressional primaries
articles.description: Zohran Mamdani's endorsed candidates achieve significant victories in congressional primaries, reshaping New York's political landscape towards the left.

articles.title: Shwetha Menon quits AMMA: 'Resigned out of self-respect, not weakness'
articles.description: Actor Shwetha Menon stated she quit AMMA out of self-respect, alleging character assassination and that vested interests blocked investigations. She demanded a forensic audit and denied political affiliations. Sukumaran Mallika also resigned in support.

articles.title: 'Country's politics utterly rotten': Cong on Maha MP defections
articles.description: Congress's Vijay Wadettiwar criticised six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs joining Eknath Shinde's camp, dubbed 'Operation Tiger'. He said politics is 'rotten' and a 'wrong message' is being sent, holding both defectors and engineers of the split responsible.

articles.title: Omar Abdullah meets Tharoor-led parliamentary panel in Srinagar
articles.description: J&K CM Omar Abdullah met the Parliamentary Standing Committee on External Affairs, led by Shashi Tharoor, in Srinagar. Abdullah highlighted the importance of sustained engagement to understand the aspirations of the people of Jammu and Kashmir.

articles.title: Mamdani Goes 3-for-3 On NYC Primary Election Day As Lander, Chevalier, And Valdez Win Big
articles.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani strengthened his political influence after all three congressional candidates he endorsed won their Democratic primaries, including two who unseated incumbent lawmakers.

articles.title: US Senate passes resolution to halt Iran conflict challenging Trump's war authority
articles.description: The House-passed measure, adopted by the Senate in a 50-48 vote, directs Trump to remove US forces from hostilities with Iran unless Congress explicitly authorizes military action.

articles.description: Rudraprayag superintendent of police Neeharika Tomar claimed that the Nagrasu incident was unrelated to the Karnaprayag clash; administration urges people to ignore rumours

articles.title: 'Say Sorry For Abusing India': Indian Woman Makes Bangladeshi Waiter Apologise For Anti-India Remarks In Italy
articles.description: An Indian influencer's confrontation with a waiter in Italy over alleged anti-India remarks has gone viral, with the group demanding an apology in the presence of police.

articles.title: US-Iran War Live: US "Very Naive" On Iran's Nuclear Programme, Tel Aviv To Act Alone: Israel
articles.description: US-Iran War Peace Deal Live News: It is Israel's responsibility to confront this Iranian threat and act against it alone, said Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

articles.description: A three-member SIT is already examining the allegations. The team may resume questioning witnesses and stakeholders on Wednesday as part of the ongoing investigation.

articles.title: Congress holds ‘Asud Morcha’ in Nanded; calls out PM Modi, CM Fadnavis for failing to address farmers’ issues
articles.description: Congress organizes 'Asud Morcha' in Nanded, criticizing PM Modi and CM Fadnavis for neglecting farmers' issues and broken promises.

articles.title: 'Trump, Modi's Trust Unlocks Unlimited Potential Between India, US'
articles.description: Listing out the breadth of bilateral engagement, Gor said the two countries were now aligned across nearly every major sector.
